text: The surgeon observes the absence of adhesions and identifies an incarcerated peristomal hernia at the induction of pneumoperitoneum. The initial step involves reducing the peristomal hernia.

The surgeon mobilizes the splenic flexure by cutting the inferior mesenteric vein and the left colic artery, ensuring the planes are clear due to the previous operation. The lateral to medial mobilization of the descending colon is completed without isolating the ureter.

The surgeon detaches the stoma and suspends the pneumoperitoneum. The head of the circular stapler is inserted into the colon for anastomosis. The pelvis is visibly free from adhesions. The rectal wall is exposed using the circular stapler handpiece, and the vagina is identified and moved away. The operation concludes with the completion of a mechanical circular anastomosis.
